sections.paragraph:
m. constr. מֹות.-

(1) death, sometimes used as personified ( Psa 49:15). כְּלֵי מָוֶת deadly weapons, Psa 7:14 יָשֵׁן מָוֶת to sleep the sleep of death, Psa 13:4. בֶּן־מָוֶת 1Sa 20:31 26:16 , and אִישׁ־מָוֶת guilty of death, liable to be put to death, 1Ki 2:26 2Sa 19:29. Poet. for the dead, Isa 38:18.

(2) the place or abode of the dead, i.e. Hades, Job 28:22 whence שַׁעֲרֵי־מָוֶת the gates of death, of Hades, Psa 9:14 חַדְרֵי־מָוֶת the utmost recesses of Hades, Pro 7:27.

(3) fatal disease, pestilence, Jer 15:2 18:21 43:11 Job 27:15, compare θάνατος, Rev 6:8 18:8 Ch. מֹותָא, and Germ. fchwarzer Tod, used of the fatal disease of the middle ages, [which was also called in English the Black Death].

(4) destruction (opp. to חַיִּים good fortune), Pro 11:19 12:28 Isa 25:8 Exo 10:17.-With ה parag. הַמָּֽוְתָה Psa 116:15 plur. מֹותִים Eze 28:10 Isa 53:9. [But see בָּמָה as to the last passage.]
